hook.text: Why do people obey? Not just because of force. Most obedience comes from believing the authority is rightful.

buildup.text: Legitimacy is the belief that a political system or leader has the right to govern and command obedience.

discovery.text: It comes from tradition, divine sanction, law, popular consent, or personal charisma, depending on the culture.

twist.text: Legitimacy is fragile. When people stop believing in their leaders' right to rule, even powerful states can crumble.

climax.text: Every political system depends on legitimacy. Force alone can't sustain power. People must believe in the system.

punchline.text: Power without belief is just violence.

quiz.question: What is political legitimacy?
options: The amount of military force a leader has
The belief that a leader or system has the right to govern
Winning an election by majority vote
